channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Russian lawmakers endorse bill to ban adoptions by gender-transition countries
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Russia’s upper house of parliament on Wednesday endorsed a bill banning adoption of Russian children by citizens of countries where gender transitioning is legal. The Federation Council also approved bills that outlaw the spread of material that encourages people not to have children.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Strike blamed on Israel kills 36 people in Syria's historic town of Palmyra, Syrian state media say
item.description: Syria's state-run media say that a strike on the Syrian town of Palmyra killed 36 people and wounded more than 50, an attack that Damascus blamed on Israel.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Pope to make late Italian teenager Carlo Acutis the first millennial saint on April 27
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis said he will canonize Carlo Acutis next April, setting the eagerly awaited date for the late teenager to become the Catholic Church’s first millennial and digital-era saint.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: After 97 UN aid trucks in Gaza are ‘violently looted’ by armed men, food prices soar
item.description: Food prices are rising in Gaza following the robbery of a U.N. convoy over the weekend, during which, drivers were forced to unload aid at gunpoint, officials say.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Pope approves new papal funeral rites to simplify ritual, allow for burial outside the Vatican
item.description: ROME (AP) — Pope Francis has revised the funeral rites that will be used when he dies, simplifying the rituals to emphasize his role as a mere bishop and allowing for burial outside the Vatican in keeping with his wishes.
item.dc:creator: NICOLE WINFIELD, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US defense chief regrets China's decision not to meet during Southeast Asian security talks
item.description: VIENTIANE, Laos (AP) — U.S. Secretary of Defense Lloyd Austin expressed regret Wednesday that his Chinese counterpart chose not to hold talks with him during meetings of Southeast Asian defense chiefs in Laos, calling it a setback for the entire region.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Australia won't force social media users to share their personal details when child ban takes effect
item.description: Australian communications minister says the government won’t force social media users to hand over personal information to tech companies, as children younger than 16 are set to be banned from the platforms.
item.dc:creator: Rod Mcguirk, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Hong Kong ex-publisher Jimmy Lai testifies he didn't ask Pence, Pompeo to take action against city
item.description: HONG KONG (AP) — Former Hong Kong publisher Jimmy Lai denied in his landmark national security trial on Wednesday he had asked then U.S.
item.dc:creator: KANIS LEUNG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US will allow Ukraine to use American antipersonnel land mines against Russian forces
item.description: The U.S. defense chief says the Biden administration will allow Ukraine to use American-supplied antipersonnel land mines to help it slow Russia’s battlefield progress in the war.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Higher energy bills push UK inflation to 6-month high in October
item.description: Inflation in the U.K. has risen sharply to a six-month high in October, cementing market expectations that there will be no further cuts in borrowing rates this year.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Marcos says Indonesia has agreed to send Filipino death-row drug convict back to the Philippines
item.description: MANILA, Philippines (AP) — Philippine President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. said Wednesday that a deal has been reached for Indonesia to send back a Filipino death-row drug convict, who was nearly executed by firing squad but got a reprieve due to years of pleadings from Manila.
item.dc:creator: JIM GOMEZ AND NINIEK KARMINI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The mausoleum holding Congo independence hero Lumumba's gold-capped tooth is vandalized
item.description: KINSHASA, Congo (AP) — Congo’s government says the mausoleum of assassinated independence leader Patrice Lumumba in the capital, Kinshasa, has been vandalized. It's not immediately clear whether Lumumba’s remains — a single gold-capped tooth — were damaged or stolen.
item.dc:creator: JEAN-YVES KAMALE and MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukrainians’ opinions of US leadership falling: Gallup
item.description: Ukrainians' opinions of the U.S. government leadership have fallen to pre-war levels after reaching a high point in 2022, according to a new Gallup poll released on Wednesday. Ukraine's public opinion of the U.S. leadership fluctuated from 20 percent to 40 percent between 2014 and 2021.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, The Hill

item.title: Argentina's Milei pivots to pragmatism in first talks with China's Xi after shaking up the G20
item.description: LA PAZ, Boliva (AP) — Last year, then-presidential candidate Javier Milei declared Argentina would not “make deals with communists” in China or Brazil, calling their leaders “murderers” and “thieves” in a bid to channel the populist energies of Donald Trump and other global far-right icons into a wi
item.dc:creator: ISABEL DEBRE, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: The dark energy pushing our universe apart may not be what it seems, scientists say
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — Distant, ancient galaxies are giving scientists more hints that a mysterious force called dark energy may not be what they thought.
item.dc:creator: ADITHI RAMAKRISHNAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Biden draws support — and GOP worry — in shift on US missiles in Ukraine war
item.description: Lawmakers in both parties are generally offering support for President Biden's decision to authorize a major shift in U.S. policy to allow Ukraine to use long-range U.S. missiles to attack sites in Russia.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Lula urges G20 members to take action to slow global warming
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Brazil's president opened the second day of a meeting of the world's 20 major economies Tuesday by calling for more action to slow global warming, saying developed nations must speed up their initiatives to reduce harmful emissions.
item.dc:creator: MAURICIO SAVARESE, ELÉONORE HUGHES and DAVID BILLER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: On board with Senegal’s navy as it searches for migrants on a popular but deadly route toward Europe
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — As dawn arrived off the coast of Senegal, navy commander Assane Seye scanned the horizon.
item.dc:creator: ANNIE RISEMBERG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: US allies accuse Russia of 'escalating hybrid activities' against NATO, EU nations after data cables severed
item.description: U.S. allies accuse Russia of "escalating hybrid activities" against NATO and EU countries after two data cables were severed in the Baltic Sea this week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: World's hottest calendar drops 2025 edition, proceeds going to Hurricane Helene, Milton efforts
item.description: This year, the funds collected from calendar sales will support those impacted by Hurricanes Helene and Milton, to provide immediate relief and aid to impacted communities.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Cruz says UN draft resolution against Israel is aimed at undermining incoming Trump admin, GOP Congress
item.description: In the closing weeks of the Biden administration, there are new concerns in Israel and among supporters of the Jewish state that the Biden administration will seek to punish Israel at the United Nations.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: India overtakes China as top source of international students at US colleges
item.description: For the 2023-2024 school year, India saw a massive 23 percent increase in the number of college students sent to America.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: Bolton: Moscow, Beijing should feel ‘a lot of relief’ if Gabbard is confirmed as DNI
item.description: Former national security adviser John Bolton said that Russia and China should feel “a lot of relief” if former Rep. Tulsi Gabbard (Hawaii) is confirmed as President-elect Trump’s choice for director of national intelligence. “She’s not capable of doing it,” Bolton said Sunday on CNN.
item.dc:creator: Lauren Irwin, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu confirms Israel strike against Iran hit nuke program during October retaliatory strikes
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu confirmed that part of Iran's nuclear program was degraded after IDF strikes on military facilities in and around Tehran in October.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Biden announces new sanctions against 'extremist' Israeli settlers
item.description: The Biden administration on Monday announced sanctions against what it described as Israeli citizens and entities involved in the extremist settler movement in the West Bank, part of U.S. efforts to curb escalating violence targeting Palestinians in the territory.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, The Hill

item.title: Russia launches one of its fiercest missile and drone attacks at Ukraine's infrastructure
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A Russian strike on a nine-story building in the city of Sumy in northern Ukraine killed eight people and wounded dozens, an official said Sunday, as Russia launched a massive drone and missile attack described by officials as the largest in recent months.
item.dc:creator: SAMYA KULLAB and JOANNA KOZLOWSKA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Cruise line offers 4-year cruise to 'skip forward,' in wake of U.S. election results
item.description: One cruise line is offering travelers a customized journey for up to four years, to "skip forward," in the wake of the recent presidential election.
item.dc:creator: Sara Filips, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Mexican president might be changing view on US as Trump win sends warning to ruling socialists
item.description: Following Trump’s victory, the Mexican government, led by President Claudia Sheinbaum, is considering reassessing some of its policies to navigate the anticipated challenges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: In a meeting with Biden, China's Xi cautions US to 'make the wise choice' to keep relations stable
item.description: As U.S. President Joe Biden and China’s leader Xi Jinping met for the last time, Xi cautioned that a stable relationship between the two countries was critical to the 'future and destiny of humanity.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Man arrested for allegedly trying to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on flight
item.description: A South Korean man was arrested in Peru for allegedly attempting to smuggle hundreds of tarantulas and centipedes on a flight back to Korea.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Israel's warfare in Gaza 'consistent with genocide:' UN special committee
item.description: Israel's destruction of vital water, sanitation, and food systems has created a "lethal mix of crises," the report said.
item.dc:creator: Cassie Buchman, NewsNation

item.title: Montana Tucker's Oct. 7 film showcases young victims' strength
item.description: Montana Tucker's film, "The Children of October 7," documents what some of the attacks' youngest victims went through.
item.dc:creator: NewsNation Staff, NewsNation

item.title: Moo Deng, Thailand's baby pygmy hippo, gets official song in 4 languages
item.description: In case you can’t get enough of the little pygmy hippo Moo Deng from Thailand, there's now an official song featuring the internet's favorite baby animal — released in four languages for her global fans.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, WGN 9

item.title: US government worker charged with leaking classified documents on Israel's plans to strike Iran
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A man who worked for the U.S. government has been charged with leaking classified information assessing Israel's earlier plans to attack Iran, according to court papers filed Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, Associated Press, NewsNation
